agent_outputs.raw: The benchmark is invalid for the implementation decision. Two leakage paths contaminate the result. Target encoding was fitted on the full dataset before cross-validation fold creation — this is a standard preprocessing leakage error that inflates CV accuracy by allowing outcome information to flow from validation folds into training features through the encoding. The operating threshold was selected on the held-out test set — even though the 80% recall target is operationally specified, the threshold that achieves it was found using the test set, making the precision estimate at that threshold in-sample optimistic. The comparison with the baseline is also not controlled: the baseline uses its original threshold from a prior protocol, while the AutoML system has a test-set-optimized threshold. A corrected evaluation with fold-specific encoding and validation-set threshold selection for both systems is required.

The AutoML evaluation has the classic preprocessing leakage structure: global target encoding before fold creation. Target encoding for assessor ID or repair shop computes mean claim frequency for each value across the entire modeling table, including future folds. When this encoding is used in training, it embeds outcome information from the validation set. The resulting CV AUC is an overly optimistic estimate. The test-set threshold selection adds a second contamination: using the test set to identify the 80% recall threshold optimizes the threshold on test data. The baseline comparison is asymmetric. These are three independent problems all pointing in the same direction — the claimed AutoML advantage may be largely artifactual.

I see two methodological errors that together invalidate the benchmark. First, target encoding and median imputation were computed on the full modeling table before fold creation. For target encoding specifically, this allows validation-fold outcome statistics to influence training-fold features. The correct design is to compute encoding within each training fold. Second, the operating threshold was identified on the test set. The 80% recall is operationally required, but the threshold achieving exactly 80% on the test set is selected using test data — this is threshold selection on the holdout. The logistic baseline is evaluated under a different threshold protocol, making the comparison asymmetric. Critique wins.

synthesis: Unanimous critique_wins across all three agents. Target encoding leakage before fold creation and test-set threshold selection are identified by all three as primary flaws. Asymmetric baseline protocol noted by all three. The convergence is complete and consistent.

etd_constraint.condition: Fold-specific target encoding; validation-set threshold selection for both systems; evaluate on independent test set
etd_constraint.supports_critique_if: Under leakage-free protocol, AutoML lift at 80% recall falls below 1 point or reverses
etd_constraint.supports_defense_if: AutoML still shows 0.02+ ROC-AUC and 3+ point precision gain under leakage-free protocol
etd_constraint.ambiguous_if: ROC-AUC gap preserved but threshold-based lift gain disappears

all_issues_raised: Full-dataset target encoding before fold creation (all three agents)
Threshold selected on test set (all three agents)
